title: Diani Beach
description: White sand, reef, and tuk-tuk hops — Kenya's backpacker coast base with hostels and street food.
highlights:
White sand beach
Tuk-tuk hops
Hostel culture
Reef access
directions: SGR Nairobi–Mombasa, matatu to Likoni ferry, matatu/tuk-tuk to Diani.
public_transport: SGR KES 1,000–1,500; ferry free foot; tuk-tuk KES 200–400.
safety_and_conditions: Strong currents some zones — ask locals. Ferry queues peak weekends.
what_to_carry: Sunscreen, reef shoes, cash for tuk-tuks.
travel_tips: Hostels KES 1,500–2,500 dorm; street chapati and mishkaki cheap.
nearby_hotels: Hostels to guesthouses along beach road

title: Kilifi Creek
description: Laid-back creek town with dhows, swimming holes, and cheaper beds than Diani.
highlights:
Creek dhows
Swimming holes
Budget camps
Night market
directions: Bus Nairobi–Mombasa drop Kilifi, tuk-tuk to creek.
safety_and_conditions: Tides affect creek swims — ask at bridge.

title: Watamu Beach
description: Marine park snorkel trips and relaxed village beach — negotiate boats at the jetty.
highlights:
Snorkel trips
Marine park
Village beach
Turtle bay nearby
directions: Bus to Malindi, matatu/tuk-tuk to Watamu jetty.
safety_and_conditions: Boat safety varies — wear life jacket. Reef protection rules apply.

title: Lamu Shela Beach
description: Long empty beach across from Lamu Old Town — dhow crossing and barefoot walks.
highlights:
Shela dunes
Dhow crossing
Empty beach
Old town combo
directions: Dhow from Lamu town to Shela — KES 200–500 pp.
public_transport: Boat only; no cars on island.
safety_and_conditions: Tides for dhow; respect local privacy on beach.
what_to_carry: Dry bag, modest swimwear away from village.

title: Tiwi Beach
description: Quieter neighbour to Diani with reef and low-key guesthouses — budget escape from the strip.
highlights:
Quiet reef beach
Rock pools
Low-key stays
Local vibe
directions: SGR to Mombasa, matatu south coast via ferry to Tiwi junction.
safety_and_conditions: Rocks at low tide — reef shoes.
travel_tips: Buy fish from beach landing mornings.

title: Nyali Beach
description: Mombasa city beach with public access and cheap food — good first coast stop off the SGR.
highlights:
City beach
Public access
Night food
SGR gateway
directions: SGR to Mombasa, matatu/tuk-tuk to Nyali bridge area.
safety_and_conditions: Watch belongings on busy beach; swim in groups.
travel_tips: Pirates Beach public section popular with locals.

title: Malindi Beach
description: Italian-influenced beach town with public sands and cheap seafood — jump-off for Watamu and Gede.
highlights:
Public beach
Seafood markets
Nightlife
Budget hotels
directions: Bus Nairobi–Malindi direct or via Mombasa.
safety_and_conditions: Beach boys sell tours — negotiate firmly.

title: Msambweni Beach
description: Untouristed south coast beach near Shimoni — reef, dolphins, and homestay prices.
highlights:
Quiet reef
Dolphin trips
Homestays
Authentic coast
travel_tips: Fewer touts than Diani — ask homestay for boat price.

title: Shanzu Beach
description: North of Mombasa island with reef and budget hotels — easy tuk-tuk from SGR terminus.
highlights:
Reef snorkel
Budget hotels
Near Mombasa
Beach bars
directions: SGR Mombasa, matatu north coast road to Shanzu.
safety_and_conditions: Strong currents off reef — go with boat guides.
what_to_carry: Snorkel gear or rent on beach.

title: Funzi Island
description: Mangrove channels and sandbank picnics — day dhow from the south coast on a budget.
highlights:
Mangrove channels
Sandbank picnic
Dhow day trip
Birding
directions: Matatu to Bodo or Ramisi, arrange dhow with local operator.
safety_and_conditions: Life jackets on dhow; tide timing matters.
travel_tips: Book via Diani operators or direct for cheaper.
